content:
Stop letting other features import your internals. A Public API is one of the simplest ways to protect your architecture as your application grows.

3 min read
9 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

It Starts with One Import
Your application has two features:
Customers
OrdersThe Orders feature needs customer information.
A developer quickly imports an internal service.
import { CustomerStore } from
'../customers/state/customer.store';The feature works.
Everyone is happy.
A few weeks later another developer imports:
import { CustomerApiService } from
'../customers/data-access/customer-api.service';Then someone imports:
import { CustomerMapper } from
'../customers/data-access/customer.mapper';Nothing breaks.
But your feature boundary slowly disappears.
The Real Problem
The problem isn’t the import.
